steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence ends with a relative clause ('who served in') that needs its object, followed by a list of two Americans. The blank is a punctuation mark that must connect the main clause to those two names.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — period: cuts off 'who served in' with no object, leaving a sentence fragment. ✗
  • Choice B — colon: a complete statement precedes it, and the two names that follow serve as specific elaboration. ✓
  • Choice C — semicolon: requires an independent clause on both sides; the two names alone are not an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice D — no punctuation: runs the clause into the names with no clear boundary, creating an awkward construction. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
